No task's scope is exclusively test-file changes (each of T-001..T-004 also touches fixtures, scripts, registration files, or docs), so the 'exclusively test-file' branch does not apply. This is attempt 1 round 2 (not round-1-attempt-1), so the applicable rule is: Approval must not be Approved, Status must be one of Planned/In Progress/Blocked. All four tasks carry 'Approval: Draft' and 'Status: Planned', satisfying the subsequent-round rule.

All four tasks declare Risk: medium and Required Workflow: acceptance-first, matching the risk-gate-matrix pairing medium->acceptance-first. Consistent with precheck-result.json workflow_match_precheck: PASS. T-003/T-004's Risk Rationale now states three explicit grounds for retaining medium (mechanical full-parity port vs. the cited high-risk precedent that changed predecessor validation logic; TEST-013/016 external self-healing observation; TEST-011/014 explicit reject-path assertions) — no Risk/Workflow mismatch found.

AC-001..016 are each claimed by exactly one task. AC-017, AC-018, AC-019 are shared across multiple tasks, but each task's Done When bullet explicitly scopes its own share (e.g. 'AC-017 for this task's twins', 'AC-018 for this task', 'AC-019 share for this task', each keyed to a distinct file pair and distinct issue number), matching traceability.md's Acceptance Mapping table (AC-017 -> T-001,T-002,T-003,T-004; AC-018 -> T-001,T-002; AC-019 -> all four). This is differentiated shared coverage, not an undifferentiated duplicate claim.

T-002's Blockers (T-001) and T-004's Blockers (T-003) both reference task IDs present in dependency-graph.json's nodes array ["T-001","T-002","T-003","T-004"] and match its edges ({from:T-002,to:T-001}, {from:T-004,to:T-003}). No dangling blocker reference.

BLOCKERS-FORMAT result is PASS, so this check runs. dependency-graph.json edges are T-002->T-001 and T-004->T-003, forming two independent, acyclic chains. Following edges from any node terminates without revisiting a node. No cycle detected.

T-001's title has no 'and' splitting distinct concerns (one suite covering both terminal-behavior legs by design). T-002's 'Deliver the canonical brownfield seed and lock check-placeholders brownfield behavior' pairs a fixture with the test suite that locks its documented behavior against that same fixture — test/verification work directly tied to the first clause (exception 1). T-003/T-004's '...(full-parity port) and extend/complete the ps1 hygiene targets' pairs the new script with the mandatory, un-decouplable TARGETS registration required for that same script's ASCII hygiene enforcement (exception 2, mirrored by the Global Constraints shared-file rule). No task combines two unrelated feature concerns.

Reviewed all Done When bullets across T-001..T-004, including the round-2 edits (T-001's merged TEST-017/018/019 bullet, and T-003/T-004's new explicit reject-path assertions in TEST-011/TEST-014 naming specific exit codes and error-message parity with the .sh original). Every item names a concrete, inspectable outcome (exact exit codes, literal terminal strings, specific field-value assertions, SHA-256 unchanged, grep results, TARGETS array membership, before/after SKIP counts, execution logs) with no instance of the forbidden vague verbs ('ensure', 'consider', 'verify is correct', 'works correctly', bare 'update <X>'/'review <X>'/'confirm <X>' without a measurable target). Each task is non-documentation-only and each includes at least one item naming a concrete verification command or evidence artifact (e.g. `tests/validate-repository.sh` exits 0, TEST-011/TEST-012 execution log, quality-gate verdict PASS).

All four task IDs (T-001..T-004) appear in traceability.md's Task Mapping and Deliverables tables (unchanged by the round-2 tasks.md-only edit). All requirement IDs in traceability.md (REQ-001..REQ-006) exist in requirements.md.
